clusters.title: Spain starts evacuating virus-hit cruise ship in Tenerife
clusters.neutral_headline: Spain starts evacuating virus-hit cruise ship in Tenerife
bullet_summary: Health officials have identified at least nine confirmed or suspected cases of hantavirus tied to an outbreak on the M/V Hondius cruise ship
Reported by BBC World, Guardian World, Al Jazeera and others

items.title: Spain starts evacuating virus-hit cruise ship in Tenerife
items.standfirst: The first group of passengers is leaving after the MV Hondius arrived in Tenerife in the Canary Islands.
items.content: The first group of passengers is leaving after the MV Hondius arrived in Tenerife in the Canary Islands.

items.title: Hantavirus-hit cruise ship arrives in Tenerife with Spanish passengers to be evacuated first – Europe live
items.standfirst: Spanish health officials says all 146 passengers remain asymptomatic as vessel arrives in Tenerife waters We have a useful explainer on the hantavirus here:While nobody onboard the vessel has symptoms, passengers and crew have been confined to their cabins in the last few days to help halt the spread of the virus, which is only transmitted through very close contact. Continue reading...
items.content: Spanish health officials says all 146 passengers remain asymptomatic as vessel arrives in Tenerife waters We have a useful explainer on the hantavirus here:While nobody onboard the vessel has symptoms, passengers and crew have been confined to their cabins in the last few days to help halt the spread of the virus, which is only transmitted through very close contact. Continue reading...

items.title: Cruise ship hit by hantavirus arrives in Tenerife to evacuate passengers
items.standfirst: MV Hondius anchors offshore carrying 146 people as authorities scramble to get planes to take them homeThe cruise ship at the centre of a deadly outbreak of hantavirus has arrived in Tenerife for the evacuation of those onboard.The ship arrived in the Canary Islands in the early hours of Sunday morning carrying 146 people, after three people died of the virus and eight more became ill. Continue reading...
items.content: MV Hondius anchors offshore carrying 146 people as authorities scramble to get planes to take them homeThe cruise ship at the centre of a deadly outbreak of hantavirus has arrived in Tenerife for the evacuation of those onboard.The ship arrived in the Canary Islands in the early hours of Sunday morning carrying 146 people, after three people died of the virus and eight more became ill. Continue reading...

items.title: Cruise ship hit by hantavirus outbreak arrives in Tenerife
items.standfirst: Hantavirus is usually spread by rodents but can in rare cases be transmitted person to person.
items.content: Hantavirus is usually spread by rodents but can in rare cases be transmitted person to person.

items.title: Hantavirus cruise ship arrives in Tenerife for evacuation
items.standfirst: Passengers with symptoms could be sent to isolation on the island, health officials warn
items.content: Passengers with symptoms could be sent to isolation on the island, health officials warn

items.title: Cruise ship hit by hantavirus outbreak arrives in Spain's Canary Islands
items.standfirst: A cruise ship struck by a deadly hantavirus outbreak arrived in Spain's Canary Islands early on Sunday as Spanish authorities prepared a tightly controlled evacuation of hundreds of passengers, all deemed high-risk contacts after three people died and several others fell ill during the voyage.
items.content: A cruise ship struck by a deadly hantavirus outbreak arrived in Spain's Canary Islands early on Sunday as Spanish authorities prepared a tightly controlled evacuation of hundreds of passengers, all deemed high-risk contacts after three people died and several others fell ill during the voyage.

items.title: Cruise ship stricken by hantavirus reaches Canary Islands before planned evacuations
items.standfirst: Health officials will begin the complex process of evacuating the passengers and most of the crew, and repatriating them to their respective countries.
items.content: Health officials will begin the complex process of evacuating the passengers and most of the crew, and repatriating them to their respective countries.

items.title: Hantavirus-stricken cruise ship arrives in Tenerife
items.standfirst: The cruise ship at the center of a deadly hantavirus outbreak arrived early Sunday off the Spanish island of Tenerife, where the process of sending most passengers back to their home countries will get underway.
items.content: The cruise ship at the center of a deadly hantavirus outbreak arrived early Sunday off the Spanish island of Tenerife, where the process of sending most passengers back to their home countries will get underway.

items.title: WHO director arrives in Canary Islands to oversee hantavirus cruise evacuation
items.standfirst: More than 100 people from a cruise ship dealing with an outbreak of the rare and deadly hantavirus are set to be disembarked.
items.content: More than 100 people from a cruise ship dealing with an outbreak of the rare and deadly hantavirus are set to be disembarked.

items.title: Hantavirus cruise passengers to be evacuated to home countries, including Americans
items.standfirst: In just hours, the cruise ship at the center of a deadly hantavirus outbreak is expected to arrive in the Canary Islands. All the passengers will then be evacuated to their home countries, including 17 Americans. Ramy Inocencio reports and Dr. Jon LaPook has more on the virus.
items.content: In just hours, the cruise ship at the center of a deadly hantavirus outbreak is expected to arrive in the Canary Islands. All the passengers will then be evacuated to their home countries, including 17 Americans. Ramy Inocencio reports and Dr. Jon LaPook has more on the virus.

items.title: Spanish island braces for hantavirus cruise ship as WHO urges calm amid protests
items.standfirst: TENERIFE, Spain — The head of the World Health Organization on Saturday made an appeal to locals in Tenerife to remain calm as the Spanish island prepared to receive passengers from a hantavirus-hit cruise ship that has left three people dead and sparked growing local outrage
items.content: TENERIFE, Spain — The head of the World Health Organization on Saturday made an appeal to locals in Tenerife to remain calm as the Spanish island prepared to receive passengers from a hantavirus-hit cruise ship that has left three people dead and sparked growing local outrage

items.title: Hantavirus-hit cruise ship heads for Canary Islands to evacuate passengers
items.standfirst: Spanish authorities on Friday were preparing to receive more than 140 passengers and crew members on board a hantavirus-stricken cruise ship headed for the Canary Islands, where health officials have said they will perform careful evacuations. The vessel is expected to arrive Sunday at the Spanish island of Tenerife, the passengers will then be transported to the airport and flown back to their respective countries.
items.content: Spanish authorities on Friday were preparing to receive more than 140 passengers and crew members on board a hantavirus-stricken cruise ship headed for the Canary Islands, where health officials have said they will perform careful evacuations. The vessel is expected to arrive Sunday at the Spanish island of Tenerife, the passengers will then be transported to the airport and flown back to their respective countries.

items.title: 7 states prepare to receive Americans possibly exposed to hantavirus
items.standfirst: The U.S. has entered emergency response mode as a cruise ship hit by a deadly hantavirus outbreak sails toward one of Spain’s Canary Islands.
items.content: The U.S. has entered emergency response mode as a cruise ship hit by a deadly hantavirus outbreak sails toward one of Spain’s Canary Islands.

items.title: What we know about hantavirus cases tied to deadly cruise ship outbreak
items.standfirst: Health officials have identified at least nine confirmed or suspected cases of hantavirus tied to an outbreak on the M/V Hondius cruise ship.
items.content: Health officials have identified at least nine confirmed or suspected cases of hantavirus tied to an outbreak on the M/V Hondius cruise ship.

items.title: 17 American passengers aboard hantavirus-hit cruise ship will quarantine in Nebraska
items.standfirst: Seventeen American passengers aboard the cruise ship at the center of a hantavirus outbreak will quarantine at a Nebraska facility that specializes in handling patients with highly communicable diseases, health officials said
items.content: Seventeen American passengers aboard the cruise ship at the center of a hantavirus outbreak will quarantine at a Nebraska facility that specializes in handling patients with highly communicable diseases, health officials said

clusters.title: Person dies after being hit by plane at Denver airport
clusters.neutral_headline: Person dies after being hit by plane at Denver airport
bullet_summary: A pedestrian was hit by a Frontier airplane departing Denver for LA late Friday night, the airport and airline confirmed
The plane, headed to Los Angeles, “reported striking a pedestrian during takeoff” at about 11
Denver International Airport said the person had jumped a fence and dashed into the aircraft's path minutes before being struck

items.title: Person dies after being hit by plane at Denver airport
items.standfirst: A Frontier Airlines plane has hit and killed a person at Denver’s international airport.
items.content: A Frontier Airlines plane has hit and killed a person at Denver’s international airport.

items.title: Pedestrian fatally struck by Frontier plane departing Denver for LA
items.standfirst: A pedestrian was hit by a Frontier airplane departing Denver for LA late Friday night, the airport and airline confirmed.
items.content: A pedestrian was hit by a Frontier airplane departing Denver for LA late Friday night, the airport and airline confirmed.

items.title: Audio captures moment Frontier plane fatally struck pedestrian
items.standfirst: The plane was evacuated because of smoke in the aircraft after the collision, according to the flight crew.
items.content: The plane was evacuated because of smoke in the aircraft after the collision, according to the flight crew.

items.title: Person struck and killed by Frontier jet on Denver runway
items.standfirst: In Denver, a Frontier jet struck and killed a person on the runway as it was taking off on Friday. The pilots aborted takeoff after reporting an engine fire and passengers rushed to evacuate. Sarah Horbacewicz reports.
items.content: In Denver, a Frontier jet struck and killed a person on the runway as it was taking off on Friday. The pilots aborted takeoff after reporting an engine fire and passengers rushed to evacuate. Sarah Horbacewicz reports.

items.title: Frontier Airlines plane fatally strikes person during takeoff at Denver airport
items.standfirst: Passengers evacuated safely after person jumped perimeter fence and walked on to runway, airport spokesperson saysA Frontier Airlines plane hit and killed a person on the runway of Denver’s international airport during takeoff, sparking an engine fire and forcing passengers to evacuate, authorities said.The plane, headed to Los Angeles, “reported striking a pedestrian during takeoff” at about 11.19pm on Friday, the Denver airport’s official X account wrote. Continue reading...
items.content: Passengers evacuated safely after person jumped perimeter fence and walked on to runway, airport spokesperson saysA Frontier Airlines plane hit and killed a person on the runway of Denver’s international airport during takeoff, sparking an engine fire and forcing passengers to evacuate, authorities said.The plane, headed to Los Angeles, “reported striking a pedestrian during takeoff” at about 11.19pm on Friday, the Denver airport’s official X account wrote. Continue reading...

items.title: Person killed after being hit by plane on runway at Denver airport
items.standfirst: The unidentified individual had jumped a perimeter fence two minutes before being struck by the airliner.
items.content: The unidentified individual had jumped a perimeter fence two minutes before being struck by the airliner.

items.standfirst: Denver International Airport said the person had jumped a fence and dashed into the aircraft's path minutes before being struck.
items.content: Denver International Airport said the person had jumped a fence and dashed into the aircraft's path minutes before being struck.(Image credit: David Zalubowski)

clusters.title: Putin says Ukrainian conflict nearing end amid Victory Day celebrations
clusters.neutral_headline: Putin says Ukrainian conflict nearing end amid Victory Day celebrations
bullet_summary: President Vladimir Putin has said he believes the Ukraine war is coming to an end
Reported by CBS News, Sky News World, France24 and others

items.title: Putin says Ukrainian conflict nearing end amid Victory Day celebrations
items.standfirst: The Victory Day celebrations didn't include a show of heavy weapons for the first time in nearly two decades.
items.content: The Victory Day celebrations didn't include a show of heavy weapons for the first time in nearly two decades.

items.title: Putin says Ukraine war likely to end soon - despite Ukrainian battlefield gains
items.standfirst: President Vladimir Putin has said he believes the Ukraine war is coming to an end.
items.content: President Vladimir Putin has said he believes the Ukraine war is coming to an end.

items.title: Putin says Ukraine war 'heading to an end' despite ceasefire violations
items.standfirst: Russia and Ukraine on Saturday accused the other of violating a a three-day US-brokered ceasefire, which coincides with Russia's World War II victory celebrations. Russian President Vladimir Putin, however, asserted at a press conference later that evening that the war was "heading to an end."
items.content: Russia and Ukraine on Saturday accused the other of violating a a three-day US-brokered ceasefire, which coincides with Russia's World War II victory celebrations. Russian President Vladimir Putin, however, asserted at a press conference later that evening that the war was "heading to an end."

items.title: Moscow marks Victory Day amid tight security
items.standfirst: Russian President Vladimir Putin on Saturday voiced confidence of victory in Ukraine as he oversaw a military parade on Red Square commemorating the defeat of Nazi Germany in World War II – a show that didn't include heavy weapons for the first time in nearly two decades. Security was tight in Moscow as Putin and several foreign leaders attended the parade, which was scaled down even as a US-brokered three-day ceasefire eased concerns about possible Ukrainian attempts to disrupt the festivities.
items.content: Russian President Vladimir Putin on Saturday voiced confidence of victory in Ukraine as he oversaw a military parade on Red Square commemorating the defeat of Nazi Germany in World War II – a show that didn't include heavy weapons for the first time in nearly two decades. Security was tight in Moscow as Putin and several foreign leaders attended the parade, which was scaled down even as a US-brokered three-day ceasefire eased concerns about possible Ukrainian attempts to disrupt the festivities.

items.title: North Korean troops join Putin’s scaled-back parade as Ukraine agrees temporary ceasefire
items.standfirst: There were no neat rows of tanks or ballistic missile carriers rolling down the smooth cobbles of Moscow’s Red Square on Saturday, as Russia scaled back what is usually a grand militaristic Victory Day parade celebrating the Soviet Union’s role in defeating Nazi Germany
items.content: There were no neat rows of tanks or ballistic missile carriers rolling down the smooth cobbles of Moscow’s Red Square on Saturday, as Russia scaled back what is usually a grand militaristic Victory Day parade celebrating the Soviet Union’s role in defeating Nazi Germany

clusters.title: Péter Magyar sworn in as Hungary’s prime minister ending 16-year Orbán era
clusters.neutral_headline: Péter Magyar sworn in as Hungary’s prime minister ending 16-year Orbán era
bullet_summary: "It's officially a new era", said Zsolt Kerner, senior reporter at Hungarian online media 24
Reported by Guardian World, CBS News, Sky News World and others

items.title: Péter Magyar sworn in as Hungary’s prime minister ending 16-year Orbán era
items.standfirst: Jubilation in Budapest as new leader invites people to ‘step through gate of regime change’The pro-European centre-right leader Péter Magyar has been sworn in as prime minister of Hungary, marking the official end to Viktor Orbán’s 16 years in power.Saturday’s ceremony – during which Magyar had invited people to join him to “write Hungarian history” together and “step through the gate of regime change” – comes a month after his opposition Tisza party won a landslide victory in parliamentary elections. Continue reading...
items.content: Jubilation in Budapest as new leader invites people to ‘step through gate of regime change’The pro-European centre-right leader Péter Magyar has been sworn in as prime minister of Hungary, marking the official end to Viktor Orbán’s 16 years in power.Saturday’s ceremony – during which Magyar had invited people to join him to “write Hungarian history” together and “step through the gate of regime change” – comes a month after his opposition Tisza party won a landslide victory in parliamentary elections. Continue reading...

items.title: Péter Magyar sworn in as Hungary's prime minister, ending Viktor Orbán's rule
items.standfirst: Péter Magyar's center-right Tisza party defeated Orbán's nationalist-populist Fidesz in a stunning blow last month.
items.content: Péter Magyar's center-right Tisza party defeated Orbán's nationalist-populist Fidesz in a stunning blow last month.

items.title: Hungary’s new prime minister sworn in after landslide defeat of Viktor Orban
items.standfirst: Centre-right leader Peter Magyar has been sworn in as Hungary’s new prime minister.
items.content: Centre-right leader Peter Magyar has been sworn in as Hungary’s new prime minister.

items.title: 'Clean break' with Orban: Hungary's Magyar to usher in 'new era'
items.standfirst: Hungary's incoming prime minister Pater Magyar is marking a "clean break" with his predecessor Viktor Orban as he is set to be sworn into office on Saturday. "It's officially a new era", said Zsolt Kerner, senior reporter at Hungarian online media 24.hu. Magyar was previously a member of Orban's Fidesz party.
items.content: Hungary's incoming prime minister Pater Magyar is marking a "clean break" with his predecessor Viktor Orban as he is set to be sworn into office on Saturday. "It's officially a new era", said Zsolt Kerner, senior reporter at Hungarian online media 24.hu. Magyar was previously a member of Orban's Fidesz party.

items.title: Magyar to succeed Orban as Hungary's new prime minister
items.standfirst: Pro-European conservative Peter Magyar arrived at Hungary's parliament Saturday morning to be sworn in as prime minister, on a promise of "regime change" after nationalist Viktor Orban's 16 years in power. The former government insider-turned-critic scored a landslide victory last month, pledging wide-ranging reforms to fight corruption.
items.content: Pro-European conservative Peter Magyar arrived at Hungary's parliament Saturday morning to be sworn in as prime minister, on a promise of "regime change" after nationalist Viktor Orban's 16 years in power. The former government insider-turned-critic scored a landslide victory last month, pledging wide-ranging reforms to fight corruption.

items.title: Hungary’s Magyar set to be sworn in as new prime minister
items.standfirst: Hungary's incoming prime minister, Peter Magyar, arrived Saturday at the Parliament building to be sworn into office, ending Viktor Orban's autocratic 16-year rule. RFI Correspondent Florence Le Bruyère reports from Budapest.
items.content: Hungary's incoming prime minister, Peter Magyar, arrived Saturday at the Parliament building to be sworn into office, ending Viktor Orban's autocratic 16-year rule. RFI Correspondent Florence Le Bruyère reports from Budapest.

clusters.title: Live: Iran's Guards threaten US sites in the region if its tankers come under fire
clusters.neutral_headline: Iran’s Revolutionary Guards threaten US sites in Middle East...

items.title: Live: Iran's Guards threaten US sites in the region if its tankers come under fire
items.standfirst: Iran’s Revolutionary Guards have threatened to target US sites in the Middle East if its tankers come under fire, Iranian media reported on Saturday, as Washington continued to wait for Tehran’s response to its latest proposal for a peace deal. Follow our live blog for all the latest updates.
items.content: Iran’s Revolutionary Guards have threatened to target US sites in the Middle East if its tankers come under fire, Iranian media reported on Saturday, as Washington continued to wait for Tehran’s response to its latest proposal for a peace deal. Follow our live blog for all the latest updates.

items.title: Iran’s Revolutionary Guards threaten US sites in Middle East if tankers come under fire
items.standfirst: Revolutionary Guards issue warning as Trump awaits Iran’s response to Washington’s latest proposal for peace dealIran’s Revolutionary Guards have threatened to target US sites in the Middle East if its tankers come under fire, Iranian media reported on Saturday, as Washington was left waiting for Tehran’s response to its latest negotiating position.“Any attack on Iranian tankers and commercial vessels will result in a heavy attack on one of the American centres in the region and enemy ships,” the force said, a day after US strikes on two Iranian tankers in the Gulf of Oman. Continue reading...
items.content: Revolutionary Guards issue warning as Trump awaits Iran’s response to Washington’s latest proposal for peace dealIran’s Revolutionary Guards have threatened to target US sites in the Middle East if its tankers come under fire, Iranian media reported on Saturday, as Washington was left waiting for Tehran’s response to its latest negotiating position.“Any attack on Iranian tankers and commercial vessels will result in a heavy attack on one of the American centres in the region and enemy ships,” the force said, a day after US strikes on two Iranian tankers in the Gulf of Oman. clusters.title: Stalemate lingers as Iran waits to respond to latest U.S. peace proposal
clusters.neutral_headline: Stalemate lingers as Iran waits to respond to latest U.S. peace proposal

items.title: Stalemate lingers as Iran waits to respond to latest U.S. peace proposal
items.standfirst: No breakthroughs to end the war with Iran have been made as the U.S. awaits Iran’s response to its latest peace proposal. Despite exchanging fire this week, both sides are maintaining that the month-long ceasefire still holds. NBC News’ Julie Tsikrin has the latest.
items.content: No breakthroughs to end the war with Iran have been made as the U.S. awaits Iran’s response to its latest peace proposal. Despite exchanging fire this week, both sides are maintaining that the month-long ceasefire still holds. NBC News’ Julie Tsikrin has the latest.

items.title: US awaits Iran's response to latest proposal amid technical ceasefire
items.standfirst: The US ​and Iran appeared no closer on Saturday to finding an end to their war after the two sides traded fire in the Gulf amid a tenuous ceasefire.
items.content: The US ​and Iran appeared no closer on Saturday to finding an end to their war after the two sides traded fire in the Gulf amid a tenuous ceasefire.

items.title: Man arrested after five people were hit by car in Nottinghamshire town
items.standfirst: One man critically injured following incident in Arnold and suspect is being held on suspicion of attempted murderA man has been arrested on suspicion of attempted murder after five people were struck by a car in a town centre in Nottinghamshire, police have said.One man suffered life-threatening injuries and remains critically ill in hospital while four other men sustained less serious injuries after the incident in Arnold shortly before 1.10am on Saturday. Continue reading...

Continue reading...
